project: Whiteboard v2.1 — Core Render Engine & State Management Refactor
description: 参考 Excalidraw 架构彻底重写画布渲染引擎与状态管理。引入 Scene 场景概念、分层 Canvas 渲染（Static + Interactive）、统一 Pointer Event 管线、SceneElement 统一数据模型，消除绘制闪烁、图形不留存、缩放脱节等致命问题。

userStories.title: Define SceneElement type system
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to define the new SceneElement type system to replace the old Shape types, providing a unified base class with type-specific extensions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create types/element.ts with BaseElement interface containing all 22 base fields: id, type, x, y, width, height, angle, strokeColor, strokeWidth, strokeStyle, fillStyle, fillColor, roughness, opacity, version, versionNonce, isDeleted, groupIds, index, updated, ownerId, seed
Define 8 element subtypes: RectangleElement, EllipseElement, RhombusElement, LineElement, ArrowElement, FreehandElement, TextElement, ImageElement
LineElement and ArrowElement use points: Point[] array instead of separate startX/startY/endX/endY
TextElement includes content, fontSize, fontFamily, textAlign, verticalAlign, lineHeight fields
ImageElement includes src (string) and fileId (string | null) fields
Define SceneElement union type of all 8 subtypes
Define DraftElement interface wrapping a SceneElement with isDraft: true flag
Define ToolHandler interface with onPointerDown/onPointerMove/onPointerUp methods
Delete the old types/shapes.ts file entirely (no backward compatibility layer)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Scene class with Map-based element storage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Scene class that manages elements in a Map, providing CRUD operations, snapshot, and bounding box calculation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create core/Scene.ts class with private elements: Map<string, SceneElement>
getElements() returns array of elements where isDeleted === false, sorted by index
getElement(id) returns the element or undefined in O(1)
addElement(element) sets the element in the map
updateElement(id, updates) merges updates into existing element, increments version by 1, updates updated timestamp
deleteElement(id) sets isDeleted = true on the element (does NOT remove from Map)
restoreElement(id) sets isDeleted = false on the element
replaceAll(elements) clears the map and inserts all elements from the array
snapshot() returns a deep copy array of all elements in the map
getBoundingBox() computes the minimum bounding box of all non-deleted elements, returns { x, y, width, height } or null if empty
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement coordinate transform utility functions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pure utility functions for screen/world coordinate conversion and canvas transform application.
acceptanceCriteria: Create core/transform.ts with ViewTransform interface (scrollX, scrollY, zoom)
screenToWorld(screenX, screenY, transform) returns { x, y } world coordinates using formula: (screen - scroll) / zoom
worldToScreen(worldX, worldY, transform) returns { x, y } screen coordinates using formula: world * zoom + scroll
applyTransform(ctx, transform) calls ctx.setTransform(zoom, 0, 0, zoom, scrollX, scrollY)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement three-layer canvas container component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to replace the single canvas element with a three-layer canvas container (Background, Static, Interactive) to eliminate drawing flicker.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the single <canvas> in CanvasComponent with three absolutely-stacked <canvas> elements
Layer 0 (Background): id canvas-bg, renders canvas background color from theme
Layer 1 (Static): id canvas-static, uses roughjs (RoughCanvas) to render committed elements
Layer 2 (Interactive): id canvas-interactive, uses native Canvas 2D for draft elements, selection boxes, resize handles, remote cursors
All three layers share the same dimensions (window.innerWidth x window.innerHeight)
CSS ensures layers are perfectly aligned (position: absolute, same top/left/width/height)
Implement renderBackground(theme) function that fills bg canvas with theme.canvasBg

Implement renderStaticScene(scene, theme, viewTransform): clears static canvas, applies applyTransform, iterates scene.getElements() and draws each with roughjs, draws colored attribution borders on shapes owned by other users

Implement renderInteractive(draft, selection, theme, viewTransform): clears interactive canvas only, applies applyTransform, draws draft using native Canvas 2D (not roughjs), draws selection bounding boxes and 8 resize handles, draws remote cursors
Window resize handler resizes all three canvases and triggers full redraw
Verify in browser: drawing a shape no longer flickers; completed shapes remain stable

userStories.title: Rewrite drawShape function to work with SceneElement
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to rewrite the drawShape function to work with the new SceneElement interface, using roughjs for Static layer rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite drawShape(roughCanvas, element, theme) to accept SceneElement instead of old Shape
RectangleElement: calls rc.rectangle(x, y, width, height, roughOptions) with proper fill/stroke
EllipseElement: calls rc.ellipse(cx, cy, width, height, roughOptions)
RhombusElement: calls rc.polygon(points, roughOptions) with 4 diamond vertices
LineElement: calls rc.linearPath(points, roughOptions) without arrowheads by default
ArrowElement: calls rc.linearPath(points, roughOptions) and draws arrowhead(s) using raw canvas 2D
FreehandElement: calls rc.linearPath(points, roughOptions) with roughjs freehand style
TextElement: renders text using native ctx.fillText with proper font, alignment, line height
ImageElement: draws image from imageCacheRef or loads from src, draws with ctx.drawImage
Stroke colors apply getStrokeColor(color, themeMode) for dark mode inversion
Fill style none / solid / hatch are all supported
Verify in browser: all 8 element types render correctly on the static canvas

userStories.title: Implement unified pointer event pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a unified pointer event processor that converts screen coordinates to world coordinates and dispatches to the correct ToolHandler based on activeTool.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace existing handleMouseDown/handleMouseMove/handleMouseUp with unified onPointerDown/onPointerMove/onPointerUp
All pointer events first call screenToWorld(clientX, clientY, currentViewTransform) to get world coordinates
Event dispatch logic checks AppState.activeTool and routes to the appropriate ToolHandler
If canvas is locked, only allow panning (middle-click or space+drag), all other tools are blocked
Selection tool has special handling: hit-testing, resize handle detection, move/resize mode entry
Middle-click or space+click starts panning mode regardless of active tool
Pan mode updates refs directly (no React state updates during drag) and syncs back to parent on pointer-up
Verify in browser: clicking on canvas with any tool responds correctly to pointer events

userStories.title: Implement ToolHandler for each drawing tool
userStories.description: As a developer, I need independent ToolHandler implementations for each drawing tool (rectangle, ellipse, rhombus, line, arrow, freehand, eraser) following the Strategy Pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Create core/tools/RectangleHandler: onPointerDown creates draft with width=0 height=0, onPointerMove updates dimensions, onPointerUp commits if dimensions > 3px
Create core/tools/EllipseHandler: same pattern as RectangleHandler
Create core/tools/RhombusHandler: same pattern as RectangleHandler
Create core/tools/LineHandler: onPointerDown creates draft with points=[[0,0]], onPointerMove updates to [[0,0],[dx,dy]], onPointerUp commits if distance > 3px
Create core/tools/ArrowHandler: same as LineHandler with endArrowhead=arrow
Create core/tools/FreehandHandler: onPointerDown creates draft with points=[[0,0]], onPointerMove appends each point, onPointerUp commits if point count > 2
Create core/tools/EraserHandler: onPointerDown initializes points array, onPointerMove appends and triggers interactive erase rendering, onPointerUp marks intersected elements as isDeleted=true
Select tool has its own handler with hit-testing, multi-select, move, resize sub-modes
Each tool updates Interactive canvas during drag (not Static canvas)
On pointerUp, committed element is added to Scene and triggers Static canvas redraw
Verify in browser: each tool button draws its corresponding shape correctly; shapes persist after mouse release

userStories.title: Unify zoom control with View Transform system
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the zoom buttons and wheel zoom to correctly update AppState.zoom, apply canvas transforms, and maintain accurate coordinate mapping.
acceptanceCriteria: Zoom +/- buttons in BottomPanel call a unified setZoom(newValue) function
Zoom step is 25%, range is 10% to 500%
setZoom updates AppState.zoom.value and triggers both Static and Interactive canvas redraws
Zoom center is the current viewport center for button zoom
Wheel zoom uses mouse position as zoom center (existing behavior preserved)
Mouse coordinate mapping remains accurate after zoom (screenToWorld correctly uses updated zoom value)
Percentage display in BottomPanel updates in real time
Pan (scrollX, scrollY) and zoom are stored as refs during drag/wheel for jitter-free rendering, with debounced state sync to parent
Verify in browser: clicking + / - buttons zooms correctly; wheel zoom works; percentage text updates; coordinate mapping remains accurate at all zoom levels

userStories.title: Write unit tests for core logic (Scene, transform, history)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unit tests for the core logic (Scene class, transform functions, snapshot mechanism) to ensure correctness and prevent regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Set up test infrastructure (Vitest or Jest) in the frontend project

Scene tests (core/__tests__/Scene.test.ts): addElement adds to map, getElements returns it; updateElement merges updates and increments version by 1; deleteElement sets isDeleted=true, element still in map, excluded from getElements; restoreElement sets isDeleted=false; replaceAll clears map and replaces; snapshot returns deep copy, modifying snapshot does not affect scene; getBoundingBox returns correct box for multiple elements and null for empty scene; getElements sorts by index field

Transform tests (core/__tests__/transform.test.ts): screenToWorld(100,100,{scrollX:0,scrollY:0,zoom:1}) returns {x:100,y:100}; screenToWorld(100,100,{scrollX:50,scrollY:50,zoom:2}) returns {x:25,y:25}; worldToScreen is inverse of screenToWorld; screenToWorld and worldToScreen are symmetric at zoom levels 0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 5.0

Snapshot/undo tests (core/__tests__/history.test.ts): pushing snapshot preserves current state; undo restores previous snapshot correctly; redo restores forward snapshot correctly; multiple undo/redo cycles work without corruption; clear canvas (all isDeleted=true) can be fully undone
All tests pass with npm test
Test coverage for core/ directory is 80% or higher
userStories.notes: Runs in parallel after Phase 1 (US-002, US-003) and Phase 4 (US-011) stories

userStories.title: Scene as single source of truth with React as view layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Scene to be the single source of truth for canvas data, with React state serving as its view layer, ensuring a consistent render(elements, appState) pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: CanvasComponent maintains a sceneRef: React.MutableRefObject<Scene> instance
Every visual update follows: Scene data changes trigger appropriate layer redraw
No direct DOM manipulation or forced pixel drawing in UI events; all rendering goes through Scene to render pipeline
pushHistory calls scene.snapshot() to save current state to history stack, then applies changes to scene
WebSocket onShapesChange diff logic adapts to new SceneElement (isAdded, isDeleted, isModified detection using element IDs and version numbers)
Remote shape_create: calls scene.addElement() and triggers Static canvas redraw
Remote shape_update: calls scene.updateElement() and triggers Static canvas redraw
Remote shape_delete: calls scene.deleteElement() and triggers Static canvas redraw
Old Shape type references are completely removed from the codebase
Typecheck passes with zero errors
Verify in browser: WebSocket collaboration works (multiple users can draw and see each other's shapes)

userStories.title: Undo/redo mechanism based on full scene snapshots
userStories.description: As a user, I want undo/redo to work reliably by restoring full scene snapshots, including logical delete/restore operations.
acceptanceCriteria: History stack stores SceneElement[] snapshots (full array copies)
Forward history stack stores SceneElement[] snapshots for redo
On any committed action (draw complete, move end, resize end, clear canvas): scene.snapshot() is pushed to history stack and forwardHistory is cleared
Undo: pops from history, pushes current snapshot to forwardHistory, calls scene.replaceAll() with popped snapshot, triggers Static canvas redraw
Redo: pops from forwardHistory, pushes current snapshot to history, calls scene.replaceAll() with popped snapshot, triggers Static canvas redraw
Undo/redo buttons in BottomPanel are disabled when respective stacks are empty
Ctrl+Z triggers undo, Ctrl+Y triggers redo (existing keyboard shortcuts preserved)
selectedIds is cleared on undo/redo
Clear canvas action (all elements marked isDeleted=true) can be fully restored with one undo
Multiple undo/redo cycles do not corrupt state
Verify in browser: draw to undo to redo to new draw works correctly; clear canvas to undo restores all content

userStories.title: Theme toggle adapts to new render pipeline
userStories.description: As a user, I want the light/dark theme toggle to correctly repaint all layers with appropriate colors, including stroke color inversion for dark mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Theme toggle in UnifiedToolbar updates AppState.theme
Theme change triggers full redraw of all three canvas layers
Background layer: fills with theme.canvasBg (#FFFFFF light, #121212 dark)
Static layer: all elements re-rendered with getStrokeColor(color, themeMode) for dark mode inversion
Interactive layer: selection boxes, handles, and draft elements use theme-appropriate colors
PNG export background and stroke colors match current theme
Dark mode color map correctly inverts dark strokes to light colors (existing DARK_MODE_COLOR_MAP preserved)
Verify in browser: toggling theme updates all canvas layers instantly; all shapes remain clearly visible in both modes
